en: be me, and he gets to walk away and stay him, you know, and who wants to be him when you
ko: 내가 나로서 걸어 나갈 수 있고, 그는 그로서 걸어 나가야 하니까, 누가 그가 되고 싶겠어? 내가 될 수 있는데?

en: can be me? I mean, I'm smart, I'm funny, I was this thing, you know, I was it, I mean,
I was this magic moment, I was the apricot dot, I was totally the poof in the relationship.
I mean, I've got more poof in one finger than he could ever have, I mean, geez, sometimes
ko: 내 손가락 하나에 그가 가질 수 있는 것보다 더 많은 마법이 있어.

en: I poof just hailing a cab, so I guess it's better to know now, so that I can go poof someone else.
ko: 지금 아는 게 나으니까, 다른 사람에게 마법을 걸 수 있어.

en: Someone who deserves me, and not some screwed up, insecure guy who can't deal with a woman
ko: 나를 받을 자격이 있는 사람, 엉망진창이고 불안한 남자가 아닌, 내 삶을 정리한 여자를 다룰 수 없는 사람 말이야.

en: who's got her act together. Now, I'm gonna end up deliriously happy, and Big is gonna
die old and alone, and I pity him, really, I pity him.
What? All right, who's gonna tell her?
ko: 좋아, 누가 그녀에게 말할 건데? 뭐라고?

en: What? Honey, you're obsessed with talking about Big, and frankly, we can't take it anymore.
ko: 자기야, 너는 빅에 대해 이야기하는 것에 집착하고 있어. 솔직히 우리는 더 이상 못 참겠어.

en: It's out of our league. What is this, an intervention?
Yes, stop her before she obsesses again.
ko: 응, 그녀가 다시 집착하기 전에 막아야 해.

en: Isn't part of the whole breaking up process that you get free rein to whine to your friends?
